prompt: Please do the task stated in `/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2_generation_prompts.md`, where the TASK_ID is `af726779`.
codex_response:
Implemented `af726779`. The rule is: take the single source row of `7`s, repeatedly keep midpoints of consecutive marked columns that are exactly two apart, and place those rows every other line below the source, alternating colors `6`, `7`, `6`, `7`, while everything else stays `3`. The Opus hint was consistent with the official examples, so I used it; the Sonnet summary had no entry. No new primitive was added.

The generator matches the task family by constructing a single top source row made of `7`-runs separated by single `3`s, with mostly short runs and occasional thicker `7` barriers, then deriving the alternating lower rows from that latent 1D pattern. Validation is complete: official examples passed `6/6`, generated examples passed `1000/1000`, and the preview sheet looks visually on-family.
